hypothesis: cu13 torch fails CUDA init on host; cu124 wheel fixes driver mismatch
notes: env-only; see build log

hypothesis: baseline reproduces stable final_int8_ttt_lora on 1 shard
notes: first full run; train ~10min + TTT eval ~23min on 3090

hypothesis: fewer KV heads → faster steps + lower KV traffic → better bpb in 600s
notes: 356 vs 342 steps; NUM_KV_HEADS 4→2 default

hypothesis: half global batch → 2× optimizer steps in same wall → better bpb
notes: 695 steps; TRAIN_BATCH_TOKENS 524288→262144 default

hypothesis: higher Muon matrix LR escapes slow directions faster in fixed wall
notes: MATRIX_LR 0.04→0.06 default

hypothesis: baseline reproduces the tuned mar28 defaults on the current system torch stack and may shift the runtime frontier
notes: 751 steps in 600.3s; total int8+zlib artifact 12.64 MB; zlib 1.4313; TTT eval 800s on 3090

hypothesis: adding one layer will use the current artifact headroom more effectively than it harms fixed-budget step count
notes: 676 steps in 600.5s; artifact 13.67 MB; zlib 1.4424; TTT eval 885s; depth alone lost too many steps

hypothesis: 10 layers can beat the 9-layer baseline if a smaller batch recovers enough optimizer steps under the same 600s budget
notes: 878 steps in 600.6s; artifact 14.52 MB; zlib 1.4259; TTT eval 886s; depth was step-starved rather than fundamentally bad

hypothesis: after depth-plus-step recovery wins at 196608 tokens, another batch reduction to 131072 will improve further before gradient noise dominates
notes: 1208 steps in 600.1s; artifact 15.82 MB; zlib 1.4270; only a 0.0003 improvement over the 196608 run, so likely near the flat part of the frontier

hypothesis: with 10 layers, reducing KV heads from 2 to 1 can recover attention speed with less batch-noise penalty than pushing batch tokens down to 131072
notes: 896 steps in 600.3s; artifact 13.40 MB; zlib 1.4319; TTT eval 861s; cheaper attention recovered some speed but quality loss outweighed it

hypothesis: at fixed 10-layer depth, a thinner `MLP_MULT=1` may free enough compute to beat the current `MLP_MULT=2` balance
notes: 1044 steps in 600.4s; artifact 11.27 MB; zlib 1.4337; TTT eval 754s; thinner MLP bought more steps and much more artifact headroom, but the capacity loss still outweighed the speed gain

hypothesis: at fixed 10-layer depth, a wider `MLP_MULT=3` may improve quality enough to justify fewer steps and a larger artifact
notes: 746 steps in 600.8s; artifact 17.43 MB, over the 16 MB cap; zlib 1.4388; TTT eval 1032s; wider MLP was worse than the anchor even before accounting for invalid submission size

hypothesis: reallocating width into one more layer via `11L / MLP1` may beat the current `10L / MLP2` balance
notes: 948 steps in 600.2s; artifact 12.03 MB; zlib 1.4375; TTT eval 823s; deeper but thinner was cleaner than pure width, but still clearly worse than the `10L / MLP2` anchor

hypothesis: trading one layer for a wider MLP via `9L / MLP3` may be the viable width-oriented frontier
notes: 829 steps in 600.1s; artifact 16.18 MB, slightly over the cap; zlib 1.4279; TTT eval 933s; this was the strongest width branch so far and materially better than `10L / MLP3`, but it still lost to the `10L / MLP2` anchor and missed size validity

hypothesis: the `9L / MLP3` near-miss may need more optimizer steps rather than a different width-depth ratio
notes: 1155 steps in 600.5s; artifact 17.68 MB, well over the cap; zlib 1.4257; TTT eval 933s; more steps flipped the score and produced the best raw `val_bpb` seen so far, but the compression story got worse, so it is still invalid as a challenge frontier

hypothesis: a mild `MODEL_DIM` trim may keep most of the `9L / MLP3 / 131072` gain while recovering challenge-valid size
notes: 1146 steps in 600.2s; artifact 15.64 MB, now valid; zlib 1.4324; TTT eval 926s; the mild dim trim solved the size problem cleanly but gave back too much score to challenge the current valid frontier

hypothesis: most of the width-branch gain may survive if the model keeps `9` layers and `131072` batch but drops from `MLP_MULT=3` to `MLP_MULT=2`
notes: 1349 steps in 600.4s; artifact 14.73 MB, valid; zlib 1.4200; TTT eval 801s; one MLP-notch trim kept the width-step interaction while fixing size and produced the new best valid frontier

hypothesis: a stronger global trim to `DIM448` may recover most of the `9L / MLP3 / 131072` score while staying clearly inside the size cap
notes: 1287 steps in 600.2s; artifact 14.19 MB, valid; zlib 1.4273; TTT eval 856s; stronger dim trimming recovered far more score than `DIM480`, but it still trailed the new `9L / MLP2` frontier

hypothesis: one less layer may be a cleaner size cut than dropping the third MLP notch inside each block
notes: 1304 steps in 600.1s; artifact 16.29 MB, still invalid; zlib 1.4274; TTT eval 836s; dropping one layer preserved decent quality, but it neither beat `9L / MLP2` nor solved the size problem

hypothesis: two light cuts together, one layer plus `DIM480`, may preserve score better than a single aggressive trim
notes: 1292 steps in 600.4s; artifact 14.42 MB, valid; zlib 1.4267; TTT eval 829s; the combined light cuts beat the other fallback trims, but still trailed the `9L / MLP2` winner

hypothesis: the new `9L / MLP2` winner may still be meaningfully step-limited at `131072` tokens and improve further at `98304`
notes: 1718 steps in 600.1s; artifact 15.48 MB, valid; zlib 1.4091; TTT eval 801s; more steps produced a major frontier jump, so the new winner was still under-trained

hypothesis: a small matrix-LR increase alone may improve the `9L / MLP2 / 131072` winner without needing a smaller batch
notes: 1348 steps in 600.1s; artifact 14.92 MB, valid; zlib 1.4270; TTT eval 801s; higher LR alone regressed badly, so step recovery mattered much more than this simple optimizer retune

hypothesis: extra steps and a small matrix-LR bump may only work together on the `9L / MLP2` frontier
notes: 1719 steps in 600.1s; artifact 15.55 MB, valid; zlib 1.4148; TTT eval 801s; the combo stayed strong but still gave back clear ground to `98304` alone, so LR did not stack cleanly with the step win

hypothesis: the smaller fallback line `8L / MLP3 / DIM480` may also be step-limited and recover sharply at `98304`
notes: 1655 steps in 600.1s; artifact 15.19 MB, valid; zlib 1.4174; TTT eval 830s; extra steps materially improved the fallback line, but not enough to catch the `9L / MLP2 / 98304` frontier

hypothesis: the fallback line may want both more steps and a small matrix-LR bump, even if the main line did not
notes: 1657 steps in 600.0s; artifact 15.28 MB, valid; zlib 1.4213; TTT eval 829s; the LR bump also hurt the fallback line, so more steps helped but this optimizer tweak did not

hypothesis: the current frontier may be over-fragmenting attention, so fewer wider query heads (`q4/kv2`) could improve quality
notes: 1652 steps in 600.2s; artifact 15.33 MB, valid; zlib 1.4079; TTT eval 813s; fewer wider query heads produced a small but real frontier improvement over `q8/kv2`

hypothesis: the current frontier may be under-headed, so more narrower query heads (`q16/kv2`) could improve routing diversity
notes: 1748 steps in 600.2s; artifact 14.40 MB, valid; zlib 1.4313; TTT eval 827s; more narrower heads regressed badly, so the E1 win was directional rather than “any head change helps”

hypothesis: the stronger frontier may have outgrown `kv2`, so less KV sharing (`q8/kv4`) could recover quality
notes: 1649 steps in 600.0s; artifact 15.31 MB, valid; zlib 1.4129; TTT eval 842s; less KV sharing helped versus the old `q8/kv2` anchor, but it still did not beat the `q4/kv2` frontier

hypothesis: the current `QK_GAIN_INIT=1.5` may be too sharp for the step-rich frontier, so a softer `QK_GAIN_INIT=1.0` could help
notes: 1717 steps in 600.1s; artifact 15.45 MB, valid; zlib 1.4143; TTT eval 801s; softer attention init stayed competitive but still trailed the `q4/kv2` frontier

hypothesis: the current `QK_GAIN_INIT=1.5` may be too conservative for the step-rich frontier, so a sharper `QK_GAIN_INIT=2.0` could help
notes: 1717 steps in 600.2s; artifact 15.48 MB, valid; zlib 1.4111; TTT eval 801s; sharper QK init beat the softer bracket, but still did not overtake the `q4/kv2` head-geometry win

hypothesis: the `q4/kv2` frontier may be output-path-limited, and untied embeddings plus a separate output head could buy enough expressivity to win
notes: 1651 steps in 600.1s; artifact 15.78 MB, valid; zlib 1.3962; TTT eval 812s; untied outputs produced a major frontier jump, so output expressivity is now a first-class lever

hypothesis: the untied frontier may still be too loosely calibrated, so a tighter `LOGIT_SOFTCAP=20` could improve it further
notes: 1651 steps in 600.3s; artifact 15.77 MB, valid; zlib 1.3938; TTT eval 813s; tighter softcap improved the untied frontier again, so calibration matters on top of output expressivity

hypothesis: the untied frontier may instead want less saturation, so a looser `LOGIT_SOFTCAP=40` could improve it
notes: 1650 steps in 600.1s; artifact 15.77 MB, valid; zlib 1.3974; TTT eval 813s; looser softcap stayed strong but still lost clearly to `LOGIT_SOFTCAP=20`, so the calibration result is directional

hypothesis: the untied output head may be learning too aggressively, so a lower `HEAD_LR=0.004` could improve the frontier
notes: 1651 steps in 600.2s; artifact 15.74 MB, valid; zlib 1.3942; TTT eval 813s; slower head LR stayed competitive and beat plain untied, but it still trailed `LOGIT_SOFTCAP=20`

hypothesis: the untied + softcap20 frontier may still want a faster output-head learning rate, and a higher `HEAD_LR=0.012` could improve it further
notes: 1650 steps in 600.1s; artifact 15.80 MB, valid; zlib 1.3915; TTT eval 813s; higher head LR improved the untied + softcap20 frontier again, so output-head learning dynamics are a real lever

hypothesis: the current winner may still be slightly overconfident, so stronger clipping at `LOGIT_SOFTCAP=15` can improve the untied frontier
notes: 1651 steps in 600.1s; artifact 15.81 MB, valid; zlib 1.3917; TTT eval 813s; tighter clipping effectively tied the current best at 4 decimals, but it was not clearly better and came in slightly larger

hypothesis: the current winner may already be slightly over-clipped, so relaxing to `LOGIT_SOFTCAP=25` could recover useful confidence
notes: 1652 steps in 600.2s; artifact 15.79 MB, valid; zlib 1.3920; TTT eval 812s; relaxing the softcap gave back clear ground, so the local softcap optimum is not above 20

hypothesis: the useful head-LR direction may be upward from `0.008`, but the true local optimum could still sit slightly below `0.012`
notes: 1652 steps in 600.3s; artifact 15.80 MB, valid; zlib 1.3947; TTT eval 812s; moving head LR slightly downward was clearly worse, so the local optimum is not below 0.012

hypothesis: the untied output head may still be under-updated, so raising `HEAD_LR` to `0.016` could improve the current frontier further
notes: 1654 steps in 600.3s; artifact 15.81 MB, valid; zlib 1.3930; TTT eval 812s; pushing head LR above 0.012 also lost ground, so the local optimum is not higher either

hypothesis: if stronger clipping and faster head updates are both the right local directions, the combination may stack into one more immediate frontier gain
notes: 1652 steps in 600.1s; artifact 15.82 MB, valid; zlib 1.3919; TTT eval 812s; the combo stayed extremely close, but still did not beat the anchor, so the local output neighborhood looks mostly exhausted

hypothesis: `resid_mix` may be unnecessary on the current frontier now that the model is better trained and better calibrated
notes: 1697 steps in 600.1s; artifact 15.82 MB, valid; zlib 1.4091; TTT eval 791s; removing learned input-stream mixing regressed badly, so `resid_mix` is still doing real work on the current best line

hypothesis: per-channel learned residual scales may be optimization baggage rather than useful expressivity, so plain unit residual additions could improve the current frontier
notes: 1685 steps in 600.0s; artifact 15.64 MB, valid; zlib 1.3987; TTT eval 793s; removing learned residual scales still regressed clearly, so the scale parameters look useful even if they matter less than `resid_mix`

hypothesis: the skip structure may help, but the learned skip weights may be unnecessary, so unit skip additions could match the frontier
notes: 1652 steps in 600.1s; artifact 15.80 MB, valid; zlib 1.3921; TTT eval 808s; replacing learned skip weights with unit skips nearly tied the frontier, so skip topology may matter more than learning the skip weights

hypothesis: the encoder/decoder-style skips may no longer be earning their keep, so removing skip topology entirely could improve the frontier
notes: 1659 steps in 600.1s; artifact 15.81 MB, valid; zlib 1.3959; TTT eval 803s; removing skip topology regressed clearly, so the topology itself helps even if learned skip weights are only a second-order effect

hypothesis: several individually losing residual simplifications may stack into a cleaner and better system, so the full simplification package could still win
notes: 1742 steps in 600.1s; artifact 15.65 MB, valid; zlib 1.4106; TTT eval 767s; the full residual simplification package failed badly, so there is no evidence that these control removals become good only when combined

hypothesis: there is real redundancy in the full K/V path, and mild all-layer latent-KV compression may preserve quality almost intact
notes: 1647 steps in 600.2s; artifact 15.83 MB, valid; zlib 1.4075; TTT eval 801s; all-layer latent-KV at 128 trained cleanly and stayed under the cap, but it lost clearly on quality and did not buy a faster evaluation path

hypothesis: the frontier may tolerate much more all-layer K/V compression than we expect, and stronger compression could expose a better size/speed trade
notes: 1684 steps in 600.1s; artifact 14.73 MB, valid; zlib 1.4223; TTT eval 795s; stronger all-layer latent-KV bought more steps and much more headroom, but it was clearly worse than both the frontier and the milder latent-KV baseline, so naive full-stack compression is degrading in the expected direction

hypothesis: upper layers may tolerate K/V compression better than earlier layers, so localizing the latent bottleneck to the top of the stack could recover much of the all-layer quality loss
notes: 1672 steps in 600.1s; artifact 15.32 MB, valid; zlib 1.4030; TTT eval 804s; upper-only latent-KV recovered meaningful quality versus all-layer latent64 and beat the lower-only placement, so the mechanism is placement-sensitive, but it still stayed clearly behind the frontier

hypothesis: early layers may be the cleaner place to compress K/V while preserving later full-fidelity attention, so lower-only latent-KV could beat all-layer compression
notes: 1674 steps in 600.2s; artifact 15.30 MB, valid; zlib 1.4095; TTT eval 805s; lower-only latent-KV improved over all-layer latent64 but trailed the upper-only version, so if latent-KV is usable at all, the upper stack is the cleaner place to localize it

hypothesis: the stronger latent-KV line may only make sense if its saved memory is reinvested into one more layer, so 10L plus all-layer latent64 could outperform the no-reinvestment version
notes: 1506 steps in 600.2s; artifact 15.84 MB, valid; zlib 1.4238; TTT eval 880s; reinvesting all-layer latent64 into a tenth layer gave back almost all the saved headroom and lost too many steps, so extra depth did not rescue the compressed-attention line
